claims.subject: dual_write_system
claims.evidence: the legacy sea system and the legacy brazil system both write to their old tables and the new unified system writes alongside. every write goes to two places
claims.object_value: legacy SEA tables, legacy Brazil tables, and new unified system tables

claims.subject: reconciliation_script
claims.evidence: picks up mismatches, classifies them, and pushes them to a queue

claims.subject: merchant_id migration
claims.evidence: that drop is mostly us closing systematic issues — wrong field mappings, timezone problems, encoding stuff with portuguese characters in merchant names
claims.object_value: wrong field mappings, timezone problems, encoding issues with Portuguese characters in merchant names

claims.subject: merchant_id migration
claims.evidence: it's the brazil per-location merchant_id thing. brazil was issuing one merchant_id per physical location and a single legal entity could have many. we collapse those down to one canonical id and we have to pick which banking instructions and fee schedules carry forward
claims.object_value: Brazil per-location merchant_id consolidation — one canonical ID per legal entity, requiring selection of banking instructions and fee schedules

claims.subject: merchant_id migration
claims.evidence: some of those we can do with rules. some need a human
claims.object_value: hybrid: automated rules plus manual human review

claims.subject: brazil_legacy_system
claims.evidence: brazil was issuing one merchant_id per physical location and a single legal entity could have many
claims.object_value: one merchant_id per physical location; a single legal entity could have many merchant_ids
